titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Postdoctoral Office, Yale University, Records
titlestmt.author: compiled by Daniel Hartwig

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday November 7, 2020 at 11:02

did.abstract: The records consist of files on personnel, postdoctoral fellows, and associates and research facilities maintained by the Central Campus branch of the Yale Postdoctoral Office.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 3-D (YRG 3-D), Records documenting academic support functions at Yale.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The records were transferred from the Postdoctoral Office, 2002-2009. Accession 2020-A-0011 contains personnel files removed from RU 262, Graduate School of Arts and Sciences, Yale University, student records.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to the materials is restricted. See Collection Contents for details.
Some records in this finding aid have been redacted, as they include student names, donor names, and other restricted data. These records will not appear in the published finding aid.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Postdoctoral Office, Yale University, Records (RU 917). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The records are arranged by accession.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Established in 2002, the Yale Postdoctoral Office consists of two branches, the Central Campus branch and the School of Medicine branch. Its purpose is to assist postdoctoral fellows and associates at Yale by processing admissions, tracking postdoctoral progress, and providing orientation about Yale, New Haven, Connecticut and surrounding areas

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The records consist of files on personnel, postdoctoral fellows, and associates and research facilities maintained by the Central Campus branch of the Yale Postdoctoral Office.
